issue_description: Customer was charged twice for their monthly plan in the same billing cycle and is requesting a refund of the duplicate charge.
Customer is experiencing no 5G signal at their home address despite the coverage checker showing 5G availability in their area.
Customer disputes an unexpected roaming charge of £45 on their bill, stating they never enabled roaming and did not travel abroad.
New SIM card is not activating on the customer's handset. Handset shows 'No Service' after 24 hours.
Customer is travelling in Spain and cannot access mobile data. Voice calls work but data shows 'Roaming not available' error.
Customer wants to cancel their contract and switch to a competitor citing better pricing. They have 4 months remaining on their contract.
Customer reports intermittent call drops during commute. Issue has been happening for two weeks in the same locations.
Customer topped up £20 via the app but balance has not updated after 2 hours. Worried the payment was taken but credit not applied.
Customer's handset display cracked after a minor drop. Queries whether their plan includes device insurance and how to claim.
Customer is confused about their first bill being higher than expected. They were on a promotion and expected a lower first-month charge.
